conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That helps, honestly. So, what do you usually like to do on a typical Wednesday evening after work?
Um, well, I usually just go home and spend time with my family.
That sounds lovely. Do you have any hobbies or activities that bring you joy?
Honestly, not really. I mean, I used to enjoy gardening, but lately, it feels like too much effort.
I understand. Sometimes self-care can feel overwhelming. Can you tell me a little bit about your job as a retail store manager?
Yeah, it's been tough. We're understaffed and overworked, so I'm constantly worried about meeting targets.
I see. It sounds like that's taking a toll on you. How have you been sleeping lately?
Honestly, not great. I wake up sometimes in the middle of the night feeling anxious.
That can be really tough. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits?
Yeah, I've lost some weight because I just don't feel like cooking anymore.
Okay. Sometimes our bodies and minds can get out of sync. Can you tell me a bit about what's been on your mind lately?
To be honest, it's just the feeling that I'm not doing enough, you know? Like I'm letting everyone down.
That sounds like a heavy burden to carry. How have you been coping with those feelings?
Honestly, I just try to push through and pretend everything is fine, but deep down I know it's not.
It can be hard to acknowledge our struggles, especially when we feel like we're supposed to be strong. What are some things that make you feel most anxious or stressed?
Um, just the thought of not being able to provide for my family and having no idea how I'll manage it all.
Okay, so it sounds like there's a sense of responsibility weighing on your mind. Have you noticed any changes in your energy levels or motivation lately?
Yeah, I feel exhausted most of the time and have no desire to do anything.
That can be really tough. Sometimes we just need to take a step back and prioritize our own well-being. What's one thing you used to enjoy doing that you haven't had time for in a while?
Honestly, I miss my morning walks with my dog – it was my favorite part of the day.
That sounds lovely. Maybe we can work on finding ways to get those small moments back into your daily routine. What's something that would make you feel more energized and motivated?
I guess just knowing that things will get better eventually, or having a clear plan for my future.
Okay, so it sounds like you're hoping to find some sense of direction and control. How do you think your daily routine could change if we work together on finding those moments of joy?
Hmm, maybe I'll start small, like taking short walks during lunch breaks or scheduling phone calls with friends.
That's a great place to start! What are some things that might make it harder for you to stick to those changes, in your mind?
Honestly, just the fear of not being able to keep up with everything and feeling like I'm failing again.
Okay, so we'll work on building some self-compassion into your daily routine as well. What are some things that you're looking forward to doing or achieving in the next few weeks?
Honestly, just making it through each day without too much stress and finding a way to relax at night.
That's a great goal! How do you think we can work together to help you achieve those small victories?
I guess just by talking things out and figuring out how to prioritize my own needs for once.
Exactly. And remember, this is your space – everything we discuss here is confidential and just between us. What's one thing that's been on your mind lately that you think might be helpful to share?
Honestly, I'm worried about letting everyone down, including myself.
Okay, so it sounds like there's a sense of self-criticism going on. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "letting everyone down"?
Just feeling like I'm not doing enough for my family or colleagues, and that they're all better off without me.
Okay, so it sounds like there might be some negative self-talk going on. How do you think we can work together to challenge those thoughts?
Honestly, just by talking things out and finding ways to reframe them in a more positive light.
That's a great approach! What are some things that you're proud of yourself for accomplishing recently?
Um, well, I did manage to get through a difficult project at work without too much stress.
That's something to be proud of! How do you think we can build on that sense of accomplishment to help you move forward?
Honestly, just by setting small goals and rewarding myself for achieving them.
Exactly. What are some things that you're looking forward to doing or exploring in the next few months?
I guess just finding ways to make my daily routine more enjoyable and relaxing.
That's a great goal! How do you think we can work together to help you find those moments of joy and relaxation?
Honestly, just by figuring out what brings me happiness and making time for it each day.
That sounds like a wonderful plan. Before we wrap up today, is there anything else that's been on your mind or something you'd like to share with me?
Honestly, just the feeling that I'm not good enough and will never be able to change my life.
Okay, so it sounds like there might be some underlying self-doubt going on. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "not being good enough"?
Just feeling like I'm not living up to everyone's expectations, including my own.
Okay, so it sounds like there might be some unrealistic expectations at play. How do you think we can work together to help you reframe those expectations and focus on your own strengths?
Honestly, just by working together to find a more positive and realistic outlook.
Exactly. I think that's a great place to start. Before we wrap up today, is there anything specific that you'd like to achieve or accomplish in the next few weeks or months?
Honestly, just finding ways to make my daily routine more enjoyable and relaxing, and having some sense of control over my life.

That's a wonderful goal! I think we've made some great progress today. Let's work together to keep building on that momentum in our next session. Is there anything you'd like to say or ask before we wrap up?
Honestly, just thank you for listening and understanding – it means a lot.
You're welcome! Remember, this is your space – everything we discuss here is confidential and just between us. I'm looking forward to our next session together.
